breach-notify-01.text: Vendor shall notify Customer in writing within seventy-two (72) hours of discovering any data breach affecting Customer Personal Data.

proposed.subject: Vendor
proposed.evidence: Vendor shall notify Customer in writing within seventy-two (72) hours of discovering any data breach affecting Customer Personal Data.

kept.subject: Vendor
kept.evidence: Vendor shall notify Customer in writing within seventy-two (72) hours of discovering any data breach affecting Customer Personal Data.

sla-uptime-02.text: If monthly availability falls below 99.9%, Customer shall be entitled to a service credit equal to 10% of the monthly fee for each full hour of downtime.

proposed.then: Customer shall be entitled to a service credit
proposed.evidence: If monthly availability falls below 99.9%, Customer shall be entitled to a service credit equal to 10% of the monthly fee for each full hour of downtime.

kept.then: Customer shall be entitled to a service credit
kept.evidence: If monthly availability falls below 99.9%, Customer shall be entitled to a service credit equal to 10% of the monthly fee for each full hour of downtime.

proposed.subject: service credit
proposed.evidence: If monthly availability falls below 99.9%, Customer shall be entitled to a service credit equal to 10% of the monthly fee for each full hour of downtime.

proposed.subject: downtime
proposed.evidence: If monthly availability falls below 99.9%, Customer shall be entitled to a service credit equal to 10% of the monthly fee for each full hour of downtime.

nda-confid-03.text: The Receiving Party shall hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence for a period of five (5) years from the date of disclosure and shall not disclose it to any third party.

payment-net-05.text: Buyer shall pay all undisputed invoices within thirty (30) days of receipt; overdue amounts shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month.

proposed.evidence: Buyer shall pay all undisputed invoices within thirty (30) days of receipt

termination-06.text: Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' written notice if the other party materially breaches and fails to cure such breach within the notice period.

proposed.subject: Either party
proposed.evidence: Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' written notice if the other party materially breaches and fails to cure such breach within the notice period.

liability-cap-07.text: In no event shall either party's aggregate liability exceed the total fees paid by Customer in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.

proposed.subject: either party
proposed.amount: the total fees paid by Customer in the twelve months preceding the claim
proposed.evidence: In no event shall either party's aggregate liability exceed the total fees paid by Customer in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.

data-residency-08.text: Provider shall store and process all Customer Data exclusively within the European Economic Area and shall not transfer such data outside the EEA without Customer's prior written consent.

proposed.if: Provider stores and processes Customer Data exclusively within the European Economic Area
proposed.then: Provider shall not transfer such data outside the EEA without Customer prior written consent
proposed.evidence: Provider shall store and process all Customer Data exclusively within the European Economic Area and shall not transfer such data outside the EEA without Customer's prior written consent.

audit-rights-09.text: Customer may audit Vendor's compliance with this Agreement no more than once per calendar year upon at least fourteen (14) days' prior written notice.

proposed.evidence: Customer may audit Vendor's compliance with this Agreement no more than once per calendar year upon at least fourteen (14) days' prior written notice.

warranty-10.text: Manufacturer warrants that the Goods will be free from defects in materials and workmanship for ninety (90) days following delivery.

subprocessor-11.text: Processor shall not engage any subprocessor without the Controller's prior written authorization, and shall remain fully liable for the acts and omissions of any approved subprocessor.

proposed.then: without the Controller prior written authorization
proposed.evidence: Processor shall not engage any subprocessor without the Controller's prior written authorization, and shall remain fully liable for the acts and omissions of any approved subprocessor.

force-majeure-12.text: Neither party shall be liable for any failure to perform due to events beyond its reasonable control, provided that the affected party notifies the other within five (5) business days of the event.

proposed.then: shall be liable for any failure to perform due to events beyond its reasonable control
proposed.evidence: Neither party shall be liable for any failure to perform due to events beyond its reasonable control, provided that the affected party notifies the other within five (5) business days of the event.
